Class DatabaseConnection

java.lang.Object
com.aptcoursework.utils.DatabaseConnection
public class DatabaseConnection extends Object
Utility class responsible for managing database connectivity.

This class provides methods to establish and close connections to the database using JDBC. It centralizes database configuration details such as URL, username, and password, ensuring consistent access across the application.

It includes helper methods for:

  • Creating a new database connection
  • Safely closing an existing connection

This class follows a utility design pattern and is not intended to be instantiated.

Author:
Heaven Gurung, Sugam Rana Magar

  • Constructor Details

    • DatabaseConnection

      public DatabaseConnection()

  • Method Details

    • getConnection

      public static Connection getConnection() throws SQLException
      Establishes and returns a connection to the database.

      This method uses the DriverManager to create a connection using the configured database URL, username, and password.

      The caller is responsible for closing the returned connection to avoid resource leaks.

      Returns:
      a Connection object representing the database connection
      Throws:
      SQLException - if a database access error occurs or the connection fails

    • closeConnection

      public static void closeConnection(Connection connection)
      Closes the given database connection safely.

      This method checks whether the provided Connection object is not null and attempts to close it. It helps prevent resource leaks by ensuring that database connections are properly released after use.

      If an SQLException occurs during closing, the error is caught and logged to the console.

      Parameters:
      connection - the database connection to be closed; may be null
